DISCOVERY 610, DEFENDANTS' MIGIRDIC SAGATELYAN, THE SAGATELYAN FAMILY TRUST'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF'S FURTHER RESPONSES AND FURTHER PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IN RESPONSE TO DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS (SET ONE) IS GRANTED AS THE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES ARE NOT VERIFIED. THE MOTION OTHERWISE IS MOOT AS THE COURT WILL NOT DETERMINE THE ADEQUACY OF THE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES ON THIS MOTION. COUNSEL MUST MEET AND CONFER. HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF IS ADVISED THAT THE COURT WOULD NOT APPROVE THE OBJECTIONS, EXCEPT FOR THOSE ASSERTING PRIVILEGE. ANY DOCUMENTS WITHHELD PURSUANT TO A CLAIM OF PRIVILEGE SHALL BE IDENTIFIED IN A PRIVILEGE LOG. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE INITIAL RESPONSES WERE A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $940, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT. NOT REPORTED.

DISCOVERY 610, DEFENDANT MIGIRDIC SAGATELYAN AND THE SAGATELYAN FAMILY TRUST'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO APPEAR FOR HIS DEPOSITION ON A DATE TO BE AGREED UPON BY COUNSEL, BUT NO LATER THAN JULY 15, 2010. THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO APPEAR FOR HIS DEPOSITION WAS A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $790, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT. NOT REPORTED.

Notice And Motion To Compel Depositions Of Deft Migirdic Sagatelyan And Levon Sagatelyan
Off Calendar Jul-15-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
LAWRENCE MANN, ESQ. for plaintiff and moving party: CHRISTOPHER CHAN
LEIANNE CHOY, ESQ. for defendant and opposing party: MIGIRDIC AND LEVON SAGATELYAN

THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHER CHAN's Motion To Compel Depositions Of Defendant Migirdic Sagatelyan And Levon Sagatelyan is granted as counsel are ordered to meet and confer to set dates for the two depositions. Levon Sagatelyan's deposition shall proceed pursuant to a deposition subpoena. Order was signed in open court. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. NOT REPORTED.

Off Calendar Jul-07-2010 Text Ruling

Notice And Motion To Compel Pltf'S Deposition And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-22-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Mark R. Mittelman, Esq., for defendant and moving party: MIGIRDIC SAGATELYAN and THE SAGATELYAN FAMILY TRUST
Lawrence Mann, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Christopher Kamshing Chan

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. DEFENDANT MIGIRDIC SAGATELYAN and THE SAGATELYAN FAMILY TRUST's Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Deposition is granted. Plaintiff is ordered to appear for his deposition on a date to be agreed upon by counsel, but no later than July 15, 2010. The court finds that Plaintiff's failure to appear for his deposition was a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff in the amount of $790, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Pltf'S Further Responses And Furhter Production Of Documents In Response To Deft'S Req. For Production Of Documents (Set One) And Req. For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-29-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY THE DEFENDANTS/MOVING PARTIES, MIGIRDIC SAGATELYAN, THE SAGATELYAN FAMILY TRUST
LAWRENCE MANN, ESQ., (415)392-4660, FOR PLAINTIFF/OPPOSING PARTY

Tentative ruling is adopted. DEFENDANTS' MIGIRDIC SAGATELYAN, THE SAGATELYAN FAMILY TRUST'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Further Responses And Further Production Of Documents In Response To Defendants' Request For Production Of Documents (Set One) is granted as the supplemental responses are not verified. The motion otherwise is moot as the court will not determine the adequacy of the supplemental responses on this motion. Counsel must meet and confer. However, Plaintiff is advised that the court would not approve the objections, except for those asserting privilege. Any documents withheld pursuant to a claim of privilege shall be identified in a privilege log. The court finds that the initial responses were a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff in the amount of $940, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported. (610/EAH)
